KPI.name: 5G NSA Number of incoming X2 setup request received from LTE eNB
KPI.lformula: NSA In X2 stp req rx LTE eNB = Number of incoming EN-DC X2 SETUP request received from LTE eNB
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of incoming X2 setup requests received in gNB from LTE eNB for E-UTRAN New Radio dual connectivity (EN-DC).

KPI.name: 5G NSA X2 setup procedure success ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA X2 stp proc SR = 100 * Number of successful EN-DC X2 SETUP procedure (number of EN-DC X2 SETUP response sent to LTE eNB) / Number of incoming EN-DC X2 SETUP request received from LTE eNB
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the X2 setup procedure on the gNB for E-UTRAN New Radio dual connectivity (EN-DC). The message EN-DC X2 SETUP RESPONSE is sent in case of successful operation.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Number of SgNB addition requests
KPI.lformula: NSA Nr SgNB add req = Number of outgoing SGNB ADDITION REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGE sent to LTE eNB for call setup + Number of outgoing SGNB ADDITION REQUEST REJECT sent to LTE eNB for call setup
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of incoming SGNB addition requests received from LTE eNB.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SgNB addition preparation success ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA SgNB add prep SR = 100 * Number of outgoing SGNB ADDITION REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGE sent to LTE eNB for call setup / Number of outgoing SGNB ADDITION REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGE sent to LTE eNB for call setup + Number of outgoing SGNB ADDITION REQUEST REJECT sent to LTE eNB for call setup
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the SGNB addition preparation procedure.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SgNB reconfiguration success ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA SgNB reconfig SR = 100 * Number of incoming and successful SGNB RECONFIGURATION COMPLETE received from LTE eNB for call setup/ Number of incoming SGNB RECONFIGURATION COMPLETE received from LTE eNB for call setup + Number of not received SGNB RECONFIGURATION COMPLETE from LTE eNB on time for call setup
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the SgNB reconfiguration procedure. The denominator includes cases when the X2AP SgNB Reconfiguration Complete message is not received until TDCoverall timer expiry.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Status Transfer failure ratio during SgNB Addition
KPI.lformula: NSA Stat transf FR dur SgNB add = 100 * Number of not received SN STATUS TRANSFER from LTE eNB on time / Number of incoming SN STATUS TRANSFER received from LTE eNB + Number of not received SN STATUS TRANSFER from LTE eNB on time
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of not received SN Status Transfer messages in time, during SgNB addition.

KPI.name: 5G UE context setup success ratio
KPI.lformula: UE ctx stp SR = 100 * Number of Ue Context Setup Response received / Number of Ue Context Setup Requests sent
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the UE context setup procedure performed by the gNB-DU and then communicated to the gNB-CU. The KPI is updated per NR Cell.

KPI.name: 5G Contention free RACH setup attempts
KPI.lformula: Cont free RACH stp att
KPI.nformula: (Sum(RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_00 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_01 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_02 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_03 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_04 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_05 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_06 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_07 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_08 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_09 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_10 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_11 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_12 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_13 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_14 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_15 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_16 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_17 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_18 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_19 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_20 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_21 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_22 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_23 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_24 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_25 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_26 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_27 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_28 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_29 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_30 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_31 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_32 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_33 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_34 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_35 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_36 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_37 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_38 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_39 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_40 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_41 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_42 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_43 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_44 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_45 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_46 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_47 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_48 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_49 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_50 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_51 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_52 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_53 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_54 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_55 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_56 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_57 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_58 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_59 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_60 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_61 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_62 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_63))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number contention free RACH setup attempts with valid UE content.

KPI.name: 5G Contention free RACH setup success ratio
KPI.lformula: Cont free RACH stp SR
KPI.nformula: (100 * Sum(RA_COMP_DED_PRMBL)/Sum(RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_00 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_01 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_02 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_03 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_04 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_05 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_06 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_07 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_08 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_09 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_10 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_11 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_12 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_13 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_14 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_15 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_16 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_17 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_18 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_19 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_20 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_21 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_22 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_23 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_24 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_25 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_26 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_27 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_28 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_29 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_30 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_31 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_32 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_33 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_34 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_35 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_36 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_37 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_38 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_39 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_40 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_41 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_42 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_43 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_44 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_45 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_46 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_47 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_48 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_49 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_50 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_51 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_52 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_53 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_54 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_55 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_56 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_57 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_58 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_59 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_60 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_61 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_62 + RA_ATT_DED_PRMBL_SSB_63))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the contention free RACH setup. The contention free RACH setup is considered successful when gNB receives RAR initiated PUSCH transmission.

KPI.name: 5G Contention based RACH setup attempts
KPI.lformula: Cont based RACH stp att
KPI.nformula: (Sum(RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_00+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_01+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_02+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_03+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_04+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_05+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_06+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_07+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_08+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_09+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_10+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_11+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_12+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_13+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_14+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_15+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_16+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_17+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_18+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_19+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_20+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_21+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_22+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_23+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_24+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_25+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_26+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_27+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_28+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_29+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_30+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_31+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_32+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_33+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_34+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_35+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_36+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_37+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_38+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_39+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_40+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_41+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_42+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_43+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_44+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_45+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_46+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_47+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_48+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_49+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_50+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_51+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_52+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_53+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_54+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_55+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_56+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_57+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_58+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_59+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_60+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_61+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_62+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_63))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number contention based RACH setup attempts.

KPI.name: 5G Contention based RACH setup success ratio
KPI.lformula: Compl cont based RACH stp SR
KPI.nformula: (100*Sum(RA_MSG3_RECEP)/Sum(RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_00+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_01+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_02+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_03+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_04+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_05+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_06+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_07+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_08+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_09+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_10+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_11+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_12+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_13+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_14+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_15+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_16+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_17+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_18+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_19+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_20+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_21+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_22+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_23+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_24+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_25+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_26+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_27+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_28+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_29+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_30+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_31+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_32+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_33+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_34+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_35+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_36+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_37+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_38+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_39+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_40+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_41+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_42+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_43+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_44+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_45+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_46+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_47+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_48+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_49+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_50+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_51+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_52+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_53+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_54+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_55+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_56+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_57+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_58+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_59+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_60+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_61+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_62+ RA_ATT_CONT_A_PRMBL_SSB_63))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the contention-based RACH setup. It is considered successful when the msg3 is received during a contention-based RA procedure.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Radio admission success ratio for NSA user
KPI.lformula: NSA AC SR user = 100 * NSA AC sucess / NSA AC sucess requests
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of radio admitted NSA users.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Number of radio admission requests for NSA user
KPI.lformula: NSA Nr AC req user = NSA AC sucess requests
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of radio admission requests for NSA user.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Status Transfer received ratio during SgNB addition
KPI.lformula: NSA Stat Trf msg rcvd R SgNB add = 100 * Number of incoming SN STATUS TRANSFER received from LTE eNB / Number of incoming SN STATUS TRANSFER received from LTE eNB + Number of not received SN STATUS TRANSFER from LTE eNB on time
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of received SN Status Transfer messages in time, during SgNB addition.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Number of signaling radio bearer SRB3 setup attempts
KPI.lformula: Nr sign SRB3 stp att = Number of signaling radio bearer SRB3 setup attempts
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of signaling radio bearer SRB3 setup attempts in gNB-CUCP.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Signaling radio bearer SRB3 setup success ratio
KPI.lformula: Nr sign SRB3 stp SR = 100 * (Number of signaling radio bearer SRB3 setup attempts - Number of signaling radio bearer SRB3 setup failures) / Number of signaling radio bearer SRB3 setup attempts
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the signaling radio bearer SRB3 setup procedure in gNB-CUCP.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Non-Stand Alone call accessibility, 5G side
KPI.lformula: NSA Accessibility NR_5020d = (NR_5004b * NR_5005a) /100
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the Non-Stand Alone call accessibility on 5G perspective. The remaining steps and NSA call setup are on LTE side and are not monitored by this formula.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Active RACH setup success ratio
KPI.lformula: Act RACH stp SR = 100 * (RACH succ CB + RACH succ CF)/( RACH succ CB + RACH succ CF + failures in RACH)
KPI.nformula: (100 * Sum(RA_CB_INIT_RESOLVED+ RA_CB_CONT_RESOLVED+ INIT_MSG2_ACK_CFRA+ MSG2_ACK_CFRA)/Sum(RA_CB_INIT_RESOLVED+ RA_CB_CONT_RESOLVED+ INIT_MSG2_ACK_CFRA+ MSG2_ACK_CFRA+ RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL+ RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL+ RLF_INI_UE_SCG_CHG_FAIL_UNSPC))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the RACH setup success ratio calculated as the number of successful RACH setups divided by the successful setups plus the failures in RACH setup.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Ratio of UE releases caused by X2 reset
KPI.lformula: NSA UE rel R X2 rst= 100 * UE rel X2 reset / all releases
KPI.nformula: (100 * Sum(X2_UEREL_OUTGOING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_OUT_PARTIAL_RESET - X2_SGNB_REL_CONF_T+ X2_UEREL_INCOMING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_INC_PARTIAL_RESET)/Sum(X2_SGNB_REL_REQUIRED_SENT+ X2_SGNB_REL_REQ_RECEIVED- RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL- RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL+ X2_UEREL_OUTGOING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_OUT_PARTIAL_RESET - X2_SGNB_REL_CONF_T+ X2_UEREL_INCOMING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_INC_PARTIAL_RESET))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of UE releases due to X2 reset among all the releases.RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Ratio of SgNB releases initiated by SgNB due to radio connection with UE lost
KPI.lformula: SgNB rel R UE lost = 100 * (SgNB rel UE lost - releases before RACH) / (All releases, exclu X2 reset - releases before RACH)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of SgNB releases initiated by SgNB due to radio connection with UE lost, among all releases excluding X2 reset.RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SgNB triggered abnormal release ratio excluding X2 reset
KPI.lformula: SgNB trig abnorm rel R excl X2 rst= 100 * Abnormal releases,excl X2 rst /(abnormal releases,excl X2 rst + normal releases)
KPI.nformula: (100 * Sum(SGNB_REL_SN_REQ_UE_LOST- RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL- RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL+ NX2CC_UE_RELEASE_ABNORMAL+ RLF_INI_UE_SCG_CHG_FAIL_UNSPC)/Sum(X2_SGNB_REL_REQUIRED_SENT+ X2_SGNB_REL_REQ_RECEIVED- RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL- RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL))
KPI.desc: This KPI indicates SgNB initiated abnormal Release Ratio for SCG split bearers, excluding the releases caused by X2 reset. MeNB initiated releases are considered as normal releases.RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Ratio of UE releases due to abnormal reasons
KPI.lformula: UE rel R abnorm rel = 100 * ue_release_abnormal / (All releases, exclu X2 reset - releases before RACH)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of UE releases due to abnormal reasons, among all releases excluding X2 reset. RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Ratio of SgNB releases initiated by LTE eNB
KPI.lformula: SgNB rel R init LTE eNB = 100 * SgNB releases initiated by LTE eNB / (All releases, exclu X2 reset - releases before RACH)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of SgNB releases initiated by LTE eNB, among all releases excluding X2 reset. RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Ratio of SgNB releases initiated by SgNB due to UE inactivity
KPI.lformula: SgNB rel R UE inact = 100 * SgNB releases initiated by SgNB due to UE inactivity / (All releases, exclu X2 reset - releases before RACH)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of SgNB releases initiated by SgNB due to UE inactivity, among all releases excluding X2 reset.RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Ratio of SgNB releases initiated by SgNB due to A2 measurement
KPI.lformula: SgNB rel R A2 meas = 100 * SgNB releases initiated by SgNB due to A2 meas / (All releases, exclu X2 reset - releases before RACH)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the ratio of SgNB releases initiated by SgNB due to A2 measurement, among all releases excluding X2 reset.RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SgNB triggered normal release ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA Tot SgNB norm rel R= 100 * Sum([Number of SGNB RELEASE REQUIRED message sent with cause equal to User inactivity]) / Sum([Number of outgoing SGNB RELEASE REQUIRED sent to LTE eNB]+ [number of UE release due to outgoing X2 Reset]+ [Number of UE release due to outgoing X2 Partial Reset])
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the NSA call retainability for SgNB initiated releases. The normal releases are considered those initiated due to user inactivity, while the total number of releases are the SgNB initiated releases and the releases caused X2 Reset. Releases initiated by the LTE MeNB are not considered in this KPI.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Total number of releases on SgNB side
KPI.lformula: NSA Tot SgNB nr rel = Sum([Number of outgoing SGNB RELEASE REQUIRED sent to LTE eNB]+ [number of UE release due to outgoing X2 Reset]+ [Number of UE release due to outgoing X2 Partial Reset])
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the total number of releases on 5G side, are the SgNB initiated releases and the releases caused X2 Reset. Releases initiated by the LTE MeNB are not considered in this KPI.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SgNB triggered abnormal release ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA SgNB trig abnorm rel R = 100 * Abnormal releases /(abnormal releases + normal releases) (only SgNB initiated)
KPI.nformula: ((100 * Sum(SGNB_REL_SN_REQ_UE_LOST- RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL- RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL+ RLF_INI_UE_SCG_CHG_FAIL_UNSPC+ NX2CC_UE_RELEASE_ABNORMAL+ X2_UEREL_OUTGOING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_OUT_PARTIAL_RESET - X2_SGNB_REL_CONF_T+ X2_UEREL_INCOMING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_INC_PARTIAL_RESET)/Sum(X2_SGNB_REL_REQUIRED_SENT+ X2_SGNB_REL_REQ_RECEIVED- RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL- RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL+ X2_UEREL_OUTGOING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_OUT_PARTIAL_RESET - X2_SGNB_REL_CONF_T+ X2_UEREL_INCOMING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_INC_PARTIAL_RESET)))
KPI.desc: This KPI indicates SgNB initiated abnormal Release Ratio for SCG split bearers. MeNB initiated releases are considered as normal releases. RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SgNB detected abnormal release ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA SgNB detect abnorm rel R = 100 * [SgNb detected abnormal releases] / [Total number of releases]
KPI.nformula: (100 * Sum(RLF_INIT_SGNB_DLHARQ_OR_CSI+ RLF_INIT_SGNB_MAX_RLC_RETX+ NX2CC_UE_RELEASE_ABNORMAL+ X2_UEREL_OUTGOING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_OUT_PARTIAL_RESET - X2_SGNB_REL_CONF_T)/Sum(X2_SGNB_REL_REQUIRED_SENT+ X2_SGNB_REL_REQ_RECEIVED- RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL- RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL+ X2_UEREL_OUTGOING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_OUT_PARTIAL_RESET - X2_SGNB_REL_CONF_T+ X2_UEREL_INCOMING_RESET+ X2_UEREL_INC_PARTIAL_RESET))
KPI.desc: This KPI indicates SgNB detected abnormal release ratio for SCG split bearers.The releases in numerator are the abnormal releases detected by the SgNB, thus not considering releases after UE initiated RLF and releases caused by incoming X2 resets.The denominator represents the total number of releases. RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL and RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL are excluded from denominator as they happen before RACH success.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Inter gNB handover success ratio for NSA
KPI.lformula: IntergNB HO SR NSA = 100 * SGNB CHANGE CONFIRM / SGNB CHANGE REQUIRED
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the inter gNB handover (SgNB change procedure) for NSA. Successful procedure is considered when SGNB CHANGE CONFIRM message is received from LTE eNB.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Number of MeNB initiated SgNB releases
KPI.lformula: NSA MeNB init SgNB rel = Number of incoming SGNB RELEASE REQUEST received from LTE eNB
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of SgNB release procedures initiated by MeNB.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Number of UE releases due to radio link failure
KPI.lformula: NSA Nr UE rel RLF = The number of UEs released due to RLF initiated by UE with the root cause t310 expiry + The number of UEs released due to RLF initiated by UE with the root cause RACH problem + The number of UEs released due to RLF initiated by UE with the root cause Max RLC retransmission + The number of UEs released due to RLF initiated by UE with the root cause srb3-IntegrityFailure + The number of UEs released due to RLF initiated by UE with the root cause synchReconfigFailure-SCG + The number of UEs released due to RLF initiated by UE with the root cause scg-reconfigFailure + The number of UEs released due to RLF initiated gNB with the root cause DL HARQ or CSI + The number of UEs released due to RLF initiated gNB with the root cause Maximum RLC retransmission + Number of UEs released by gNB due to synchReconfigFailure-SCG and expired RA Timer
KPI.nformula: (Sum(RLF_INITIATED_UE_T310_EXPIRY+ RLF_INITIATED_UE_RACH_FAIL+ RLF_INITIATED_UE_MAX_RLC_RETX+ RLF_INITIATED_UE_SRB_INTGRTY_F+ RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_CHGE_FAIL+ RLF_INITIATED_UE_SCG_RECNF_F+ RLF_INIT_SGNB_DLHARQ_OR_CSI+ RLF_INIT_SGNB_MAX_RLC_RETX+ RLF_INI_UE_SCG_CHG_FAIL_UNSPC))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of UEs released due to radio link failure for NSA users.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Inter gNB handover attempts for NSA
KPI.lformula: IntergNB HO att NSA = SGNB CHANGE REQUIRED
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of inter gNB handover attempts (SgNB change procedure) for NSA. Number of attempts is considered when SGNB CHANGE REQUIRED message is sent by the source gNB.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SN Initiated Intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change execution success ratio
KPI.lformula: 100 * Successful intra gNB intra frequency Primary SCell change executions / Attempted intra gNB intra frequency Primary SCell change executions
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of intra-gNB (both Intra-DU and Inter-DU) intra-frequency PSCell change for execution phase.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change execution attempts
KPI.lformula: Intra gNB intra freq PSCell chg exec att = Number of intra gNB intra frequency PS-Cell change attempts
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of intra gNB intra frequency Primary SCell change attempts.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Intra-gNB intra-frequency PSCell change preparation attempts
KPI.lformula: NSA Intra-gNB intra-freq PSCell chg prep att = The number of intra-gNB intra-frequency PSCell change attempts + The number of intra-gNB intra-frequency PSCell change attempts with usage of NSA NR SRB3 + The number of intra-gNB intra-freq PSCell change failures due to target cell resource alloc failure
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of intra-gNB intra-frequency Primary SCell change preparation attempts.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change preparation success ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA Intra gNB intra freq PSCell chg prep SR = 100 * The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts + The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts with usage of NSA NR SRB3/The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts + The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts with usage of NSA NR SRB3 + The number of intra gNB intra freq PSCell change failures due to target cell resource alloc failure
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of intra gNB intra frequency Primary SCell change preparation phase. The preparation phase is considered successful if it originates an execution attempt. The failed preparations are given by counter INTRA_FR_PSCEL_CH_FAI_RES_ALLO.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SN Initiated Intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change total success ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA InagNB inafreq PSCell chg tot SR = 100 * The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts + The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts with usage of NSA NR SRB3 - The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change failures due to TDCoverall timer expiry - The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change failures due to MeNB refusal for reasons other than 'mcg-mobility' / The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts + The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts with usage of NSA NR SRB3 + The number of intra gNB intra freq PSCell change failures due to target cell resource alloc failure
KPI.nformula: 100 * Sum(INTRA_FR_PSCEL_CH_ATTEMPT + INT_FR_PSCEL_CHA_ATT_SRB3 - INTRA_FR_PSCEL_CH_FAI_TDC_EXP - (INTRA_FR_PSCEL_CH_FAI_MENB_REF - SN_INIT_IAG_IAF_PC_REF_MOB)) / Sum(INTRA_FR_PSCEL_CH_ATTEMPT + INT_FR_PSCEL_CHA_ATT_SRB3 + INTRA_FR_PSCEL_CH_FAI_RES_ALLO)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of intra-gNB (both Intra-DU and Inter-DU) intra-frequency PSCell change for both preparation and execution phases.

KPI.name: 5G NSA SN Initiated Intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change total failure ratio
KPI.lformula: NSA Intra gNB intra freq PSCell chg FR = 100 * The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change failures due to TDCoverall timer expiry + The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change failures due to MeNB refusal for reasons other than 'mcg-mobility' + The number of intra gNB intra freq PSCell change failures due to target cell resource alloc failure/The number of intra gNB intra freq PSCell change failures due to target cell resource alloc failure + The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts + The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts with usage of NSA NR SRB3
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the overall failure ratio of the intra-gNB (both Intra-DU and Inter-DU) intra-frequency PSCell change for both preparation and execution phases. The failure can be due to AC, TDCoverall timer expiry, MeNB refusal (different than 'mcg-mobility'). In denominator, the counter INTRA_FR_PSCEL_CH_FAI_RES_ALLO represents the preparation attempts that failed in the AC, counters INTRA_FR_PSCEL_CH_ATTEMPT + INT_FR_PSCEL_CHA_ATT_SRB3 represents the execution attempts after successful preparation.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Average duration of executed intra gNB intra frequency PSCell changes
KPI.lformula: NSA Intra gNB intra freq PSCell chg dur avg = The total duration of executed intra gNB intra frequency PSCell changes /10 / The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts + The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change attempts with usage of NSA NR SRB3 - The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change failures due to MeNB refusal - The number of intra gNB intra frequency PSCell change failures due to TDCoverall timer expiry
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average duration of executed intra-gNB intra-frequency PSCell changes.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Number of intra frequency intra DU PSCell change execution attempts for NSA 3x
KPI.lformula: Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec att = Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec att via MeNB + Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec att via SRB3
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the number of intra gNB intra frequency intra DU PS-Cell change attempts for NSA 3x.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Number of intra frequency intra DU PSCell change preparation attempts for NSA 3x
KPI.lformula: Inafreq inaDU PSC change prep att = Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation successes + Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation failures = Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change execution attempts + Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation failures
KPI.desc: This KPI shows total number of preparation attempts of intra frequency intra DU PSCell change for NSA 3x.The preparation attempts are calculated as the successful preparations (those that trigger a handover execution) plus the preparation failures.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Intra-frequency intra-DU PSCell change preparation success ratio
KPI.lformula: Inafreq inaDU PSC change prep SR = 100 * Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation successes / (Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation successes + Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation failures) = 100 * Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change execution attempts / (Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change execution attempts + Total Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation failures)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the preparation phase of intra-frequency intra-DU PSCell change for NSA 3x.The successful preparations are those that trigger a handover execution, given by INTRA_DU_HO_ATT_NSA_MNB+ INTRA_DU_HO_ATT_NSA_SRB3.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Intra frequency intra DU PSCell change execution success ratio
KPI.lformula: Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec SR = 100 * Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec succs / Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec att = 100 * (Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec attempts - Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec failures) / Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec att
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the success ratio of the execution phase of intra frequency intra DU PSCell change for NSA 3x.The successful executions are derived from the execution attempts minus the execution failures.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Intra-frequency intra-DU PSCell change total success ratio
KPI.lformula: Inafreq inaDU PSC chg tot SR = 100 * Inafreq inaDU PSC change execution successes / Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation attempts = 100 * (Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec attempts - Inafreq inaDU PSC change exec failures) / (Inafreq inaDU PSC change execution attempts + Inafreq inaDU PSC change preparation failures)
KPI.nformula: 100 * Sum(INTRA_DU_HO_ATT_NSA_MNB+ INTRA_DU_HO_ATT_NSA_SRB3- INTRA_DU_HO_FAI_T304_EXP_NSA- INTRA_DU_HO_FAI_TDC_EXP- INTRA_DU_HOPRE_FAI_MENB_REF)/Sum(INTRA_DU_HO_ATT_NSA_MNB+ INTRA_DU_HO_ATT_NSA_SRB3+ INTRA_DU_HOPRE_E1_FAI_NSA+ INTRA_DU_HOPRE_F1_FAI_NSA+ INTRA_DU_HOPRE_FAI_RES_NSA)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the total Intra-frequency intra-DU PSCell change success ratio covering the preparation and the execution phase. It is calculated as the successful executions divided by the preparation attempts.

KPI.name: 5G Average estimated F1 round trip delay between CU and MAC DU
KPI.lformula: Avg F1 rnd trip delay CU MAC DU
KPI.nformula: Sum(0.5*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_01)+ 1.5*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_02)+ 2.5*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_03)+ 4*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_04)+ 7.5*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_05)+ 12.5*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_06)+ 17.5*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_07)+ 35*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_08)+ 50*(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_09))/Sum(EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_01+ EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_02+ EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_03+ EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_04+ EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_05+ EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_06+ EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_07+ EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_08+ EST_F1_PDU_DELV_DELAY_BIN_09)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average estimated F1 round trip delay between CU and MAC DU, measured on gNB and per DU. It is the time:- Starting when the PDCP PDU packet is sent by CU over F1 (PDCP PDU packet has left CU)Then, this PDCP PDU packet is going until MAC PDU (of DU). When the 1st segment is ready to be sent on the air for the first time (excluding repetitions), the MAC PDU sends a DDDS to CU. - And ending when the DDDS (sent by the MAC PDU) message is received by CU over F1 to acknowledge the packet (removed from CU buffer).Note: - We can consider that the time to process the DDDS message is equivalent to a PDU message received on CU over F1 and the PDU was sent by MAC PDU.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Average estimated X2 round trip delay between CU and MAC eNB
KPI.lformula: NSA Avg X2 rnd trip delay CU MAC eNB
KPI.nformula: Sum(0.5*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_01)+ 1.5*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_02)+ 2.5*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_03)+ 4*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_04)+ 7.5*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_05)+ 12.5*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_06)+ 17.5*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_07)+ 35*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_08)+ 50*(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_09))/Sum(EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_01+ EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_02+ EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_03+ EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_04+ EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_05+ EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_06+ EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_07+ EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_08+ EST_PDU_DELV_DELAY_X2_BIN_09)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average estimated X2 round trip delay between CU and MAC eNB cell, measured on gNB and per X2. It is the time: - starting when the PDCP PDU packet is sent by CU over X2 until RLC multiplexed packet in the MAC PDU (of cell of eNB) to be transmitted in the air for the first time - and ending when the DDDS (sent by the MAC PDU) message is received by CU over X2 to acknowledge the packet (removed from CU buffer).Note: We can consider that the time to process the DDDS message is equivalent to a PDU message received on CU over X2 and the PDU was sent by MAC PDU (of cell of eNB).

KPI.name: 5G Initial BLER in downlink transmissions in PDSCH
KPI.lformula: Init BLER DL PDSCH tx = 100*(Number of unsuccessful initial DL TB transmissions)/ (Total number of initial DL TB transmissions)
KPI.nformula: ((100*Sum(PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_00+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_01+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_02+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_03+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_04+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_05+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_06+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_07+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_08+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_09+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_10+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_11+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_12+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_13+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_14+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_15+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_16+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_17+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_18+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_19+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_20+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_21+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_22+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_23+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_24+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_25+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_26+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_05+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_06+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_07+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_08+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_09+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_10+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_11+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_12+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_01+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_02+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_03+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_04+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_05+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_06+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_07+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_15+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_16+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_17+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_18+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_19+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_20+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_21+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_22+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_23+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_24+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_25+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_26+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_27+ PDSCH_INITX_LOWSE_MCS_28- (PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_00+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_01+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_02+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_03+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_04+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_05+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_06+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_07+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_08+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_09+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_10+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_11+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_12+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_13+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_14+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_15+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_16+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_17+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_18+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_19+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_20+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_21+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_22+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_23+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_24+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_25+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_26+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_27+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_64TBL_MCS_28+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_00+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_01+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_02+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_03+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_04+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_05+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_06+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_07+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_08+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_09+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_10+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_11+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_12+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_19+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_20+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_21+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_22+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_23+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_24+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_25+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_26+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_256TBL_MCS_27+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_00+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_01+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_02+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_03+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_04+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_05+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_06+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_07+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_08+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_09+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_10+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_11+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_12+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_13+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_14+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_15+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_16+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_17+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_18+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_19+ PDSCH_SUCC_INI_LOWSE_MCS_20+ KPI.desc: This KPI shows the Initial Block Error Ratio calculated as the failure ratio for the first HARQ transmission for PDSCH. It considers transmissions with 64QAM MCS, LowSE MCS and 256QAM MCS. HARQ feedback DTX is considered as unsuccessful.

KPI.name: 5G Residual Block Error Ratio (BLER) for PDSCH
KPI.lformula: Resid BLER DL = 100 * (transmissions failed after max retry) / (successful transmissions + transmissions failed after max retry)

KPI.desc: This KPI shows the residual Block Error Ratio (BLER) for PDSCH. Residual BLER is defined as the ratio between the number of failed transmissions after the MAX HARQ retransmission reached and the number of packets to transmit, that is, the 1st HARQ transmissions.In real-time monitoring, the HARQ feedback might be received in a different measurement period than the initial transmission. For that reason, the initial transmissions in the formula are calculated as the successful transmissions plus the unsuccessful transmissions.HARQ feedback DTX is considered as unsuccessful.

KPI.name: 5G Initial BLER in PUSCH
KPI.lformula: 100 * (initial transmissions failed) / (initial transmissions sent) =100 * (initial transmissions sent - initial transmissions success) / (initial transmissions sent)

KPI.desc: This KPI shows the failure ratio for the first HARQ transmission for PUSCH.

KPI.name: 5G Residual BLER in PUSCH
KPI.lformula: 100 * (transmissions failed after max retry) / (initial transmissions sent) =100 * (transmissions failed after max retry) / (successful transmissions + transmissions failed after max retry)

KPI.desc: This KPI shows the residual Block Error Ratio (BLER) for PUSCH. Residual BLER is defined as the ratio between the number of failed transmissions after the MAX HARQ retransmission reached and the number of packets to transmit, that is, the 1st HARQ transmissions.In real-time monitoring, the HARQ feedback might be received in a different measurement period than the initial transmission. For that reason, the initial transmissions in the formula are calculated as the successful transmissions plus the unsuccessful transmissions.

KPI.name: 5G Average wideband CQI, 64QAM table

UE_RSSI_PUCCH_LEVEL_37+ UE_RSSI_PUCCH_LEVEL_38+ UE_RSSI_PUCCH_LEVEL_39+ UE_RSSI_PUCCH_LEVEL_40+ UE_RSSI_PUCCH_LEVEL_41+ UE_RSSI_PUCCH_LEVEL_42+ UE_RSSI_PUCCH_LEVEL_43+ UE_RSSI_PUCCH_LEVEL_44))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average UE related Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) distribution for PUCCH, measured in the gNB.

KPI.name: 5G Average MCS used in downlink for PDSCH with 64QAM table
KPI.lformula: Sum for all MCS(MCSx number * packets sent in DL with MCS x and 64QAM table)/ All packets sent in DL with 64QAM table
KPI.nformula: Sum(0 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_00+ 1 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_01+ 2 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_02+ 3 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_03+ 4 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_04+ 5 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_05+ 6 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_06+ 7 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_07+ 8 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_08+ 9 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_09+ 10 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_10+ 11 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_11+ 12 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_12+ 13 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_13+ 14 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_14+ 15 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_15+ 16 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_16+ 17 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_17+ 18 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_18+ 19 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_19+ 20 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_20+ 21 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_21+ 22 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_22+ 23 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_23+ 24 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_24+ 25 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_25+ 26 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_26+ 27 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_27+ 28 * PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_28)/Sum(PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_00+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_01+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_02+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_03+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_04+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_05+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_06+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_07+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_08+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_09+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_10+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_11+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_12+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_13+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_14+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_15+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_16+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_17+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_18+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_19+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_20+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_21+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_22+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_23+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_24+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_25+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_26+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_27+ PDSCH_INITX_64TBL_MCS_28)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average MCS used for downlink data transmissions over PDSCH with 64QAM MCS table.

KPI.name: 5G Average MCS used in downlink for PDSCH with 256QAM table
KPI.lformula: Sum for all MCS(MCSx number * packets sent with MCS x and 256QAM table)/ All packets sent with 256QAM table
KPI.nformula: Sum(0 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_00+ 1 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_01+ 2 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_02+ 3 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_03+ 4 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_04+ 5 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_05+ 6 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_06+ 7 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_07+ 8 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_08+ 9 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_09+ 10 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_10+ 11 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_11+ 12 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_12+ 13 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_13+ 14 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_14+ 15 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_15+ 16 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_16+ 17 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_17+ 18 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_18+ 19 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_19+ 20 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_20+ 21 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_21+ 22 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_22+ 23 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_23+ 24 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_24+ 25 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_25+ 26 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_26+ 27 * PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_27)/Sum(PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_00+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_01+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_02+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_03+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_04+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_05+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_06+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_07+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_08+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_09+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_10+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_11+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_12+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_13+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_14+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_15+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_16+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_17+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_18+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_19+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_20+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_21+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_22+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_23+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_24+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_25+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_26+ PDSCH_INI_256TBL_MCS_27)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average MCS used for downlink data transmissions over PDSCH with 256QAM MCS table.

KPI.name: 5G Average rank used in downlink
KPI.lformula: For all RANKs (RANKx number * Packets transmitted with RANKx)/ All Packets transmitted
KPI.nformula: Sum(1 * PDSCH_2TX_RI_01+ 2 * PDSCH_2TX_RI_02+ 1 * PDSCH_4TX_RI_01+ 2 * PDSCH_4TX_RI_02+ 3 * PDSCH_4TX_RI_03+ 4 * PDSCH_4TX_RI_04)/Sum(PDSCH_2TX_RI_01+ PDSCH_2TX_RI_02+ PDSCH_4TX_RI_01+ PDSCH_4TX_RI_02+ PDSCH_4TX_RI_03+ PDSCH_4TX_RI_04)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average rank used in downlink transmissions.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Inter-gNB PSCell change preparation attempt on target gNB
KPI.lformula: IntergNB PSC chng prep att
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the inter-gNB PSCell change preparation attempts measured in the target gNB. The preparation attempts are the number of X2AP: SGNB ADDITION REQUEST messages received from LTE eNB upon SN Change trigger.Intra-frequency and inter-frequency PSCell changes are taken into account.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Inter-gNB PSCell change preparation success ratio on target gNB
KPI.lformula: IntergNB PSC chng prep att tgNB = Number of SGNB ADDITION REQUEST received from LTE eNB upon SN Change trigger
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the inter-gNB PSCell change preparation success ratio measured in the target gNB. The preparation attempts are the number of X2AP: SGNB ADDITION REQUEST messages received from LTE eNB upon SN Change trigger. The successful preparations are given by the number of X2AP: SGNB ADDITION ACKNOWLEDGE sent by target gNB to LTE eNB, upon SN Change trigger.Intra-frequency and inter-frequency PSCell changes are taken into account.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Inter-gNB PSCell change execution attempts on target gNB
KPI.lformula: IntergNB PSC chng exec att tgNB = Number of SGNB ADDITION ACKNOWLEDGE sent to LTE eNB) upon SN Change trigger
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the inter-gNB PSCell change execution attempts calculated on the target gNB. The execution attempts are considered equal to the preparation successes, given by the number of X2AP: SGNB ADDITION ACKNOWLEDGE sent by target gNB to LTE eNB, upon SN Change trigger.Intra-frequency and inter-frequency PSCell changes are taken into account.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Inter-gNB PSCell change execution success ratio on target gNB
KPI.lformula: IntergNB PSC chng exec SR tgNB = 100 * IntergNB PSC chng exec succ / IntergNB PSC chng exec att
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the inter-gNB PSCell change execution success ratio calculated on the target gNB. The execution is considered successful when target gNB receives the X2AP: SGNB RECONFIGURATION COMPLETE sent by LTE eNB (with Configuration successfully applied IE) upon SN Change trigger. The execution attempts are given by the number of X2AP: SGNB ADDITION REQUEST ACKNOWLEDGE sent to LTE eNB, upon SN Change trigger.Intra-frequency and inter-frequency PSCell changes are taken into account.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Inter-gNB PSCell change total success ratio on target gNB
KPI.lformula: IntergNB PSC chng tot SR tgNB = 100 * IntergNB PSC chng exec succ / IntergNB PSC chng prep att
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the inter-gNB PSCell change total success ratio calculated in the target gNB. The total success ratio is calculated from the successful execution over the preparation attempts. The preparation attempts are the number of X2AP: SGNB ADDITION REQUEST messages received from LTE eNB upon SN Change trigger. The execution successes are given by the number of X2AP: SGNB RECONFIGURATION COMPLETE messages (with Configuration successfully applied IE) sent by LTE eNB to the target gNB, upon SN Change trigger.Intra-frequency and inter-frequency PSCell changes are taken into account.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Admission control rejection ratio due to lack of PUCCH resources
KPI.lformula: 100 * NSA admission rejection due to lack of PUCCH resources / NSA admission requests
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the rejection ratio in admission control due to lack of PUCCH resources, in NSA mode.

KPI.name: 5G Average MCS used in uplink for PUSCH with 64QAM table
KPI.lformula: Sum for all MCS(MCSx number * packets sent in UL with MCS x and 64QAM table)/ All packets sent in UL with 64QAM table
KPI.nformula: (0 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_00 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_00)+ 1 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_01 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_01)+ 2 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_02 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_02)+ 3 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_03 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_03)+ 4 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_04 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_04)+ 5 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_05 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_05)+ 6 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_06 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_06)+ 7 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_07 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_07)+ 8 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_08 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_08)+ 9 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_09 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_09)+ 10 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_10 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_10)+ 11 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_11 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_11)+ 12 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_12 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_12)+ 13 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_13 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_13)+ 14 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_14 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_14)+ 15 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_15 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_15)+ 16 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_16 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_16)+ 17 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_17 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_17)+ 18 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_18 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_18)+ 19 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_19 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_19)+ 20 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_20 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_20)+ 21 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_21 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_21)+ 22 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_22 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_22)+ 23 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_23 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_23)+ 24 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_24 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_24)+ 25 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_25 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_25)+ 26 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_26 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_26)+ 27 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_27 + PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_27)+ 28 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_28))/Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_00+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_01+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_02+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_03+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_04+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_05+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_06+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_07+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_08+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_09+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_10+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_11+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_12+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_13+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_14+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_15+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_16+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_17+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_18+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_19+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_20+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_21+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_22+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_23+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_24+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_25+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_26+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_27+ PUSCH_CP_INI_64TBL_MCS_28+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_00+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_01+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_02+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_03+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_04+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_05+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_06+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_07+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_08+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_09+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_10+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_11+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_12+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_13+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_14+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_15+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_16+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_17+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_18+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_19+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_20+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_21+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_22+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_23+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_24+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_25+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_26+ PUSCH_DFT_INI_64TBL_MCS_27)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average MCS used for uplink data transmissions over PUSCH with 64QAM MCS table.

KPI.name: 5G Average number of activated SCells in downlink
KPI.lformula: Sum x(0 to 7) (x * Number of UEs with x SCell activated in DL)/(Number of UEs with x SCell activeted in DL)
KPI.nformula: (Sum(PSCELL_UE_1_ACT_SCELL_DL+ 2 * PSCELL_UE_2_ACT_SCELL_DL+ 3 * PSCELL_UE_3_ACT_SCELL_DL+ 4 * PSCELL_UE_4_ACT_SCELL_DL+ 5 * PSCELL_UE_5_ACT_SCELL_DL+ 6 * PSCELL_UE_6_ACT_SCELL_DL+ 7 * PSCELL_UE_7_ACT_SCELL_DL)/Sum(PSCELL_UE_0_ACT_SCELL_DL+ PSCELL_UE_1_ACT_SCELL_DL+ PSCELL_UE_2_ACT_SCELL_DL+ PSCELL_UE_3_ACT_SCELL_DL+ PSCELL_UE_4_ACT_SCELL_DL+ PSCELL_UE_5_ACT_SCELL_DL+ PSCELL_UE_6_ACT_SCELL_DL+ PSCELL_UE_7_ACT_SCELL_DL))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average number of activated SCells in downlink.

KPI.name: 5G Average number of activated SCells in uplink
KPI.lformula: Sum x(0 to 7) (x * Number of UEs with x SCell activated in UL)/(Number of UEs with x SCell activeted in UL)
KPI.nformula: (Sum(PSCELL_UE_1_ACT_SCELL_UL+ 2 * PSCELL_UE_2_ACT_SCELL_UL+ 3 * PSCELL_UE_3_ACT_SCELL_UL+ 4 * PSCELL_UE_4_ACT_SCELL_UL+ 5 * PSCELL_UE_5_ACT_SCELL_UL+ 6 * PSCELL_UE_6_ACT_SCELL_UL+ 7 * PSCELL_UE_7_ACT_SCELL_UL)/Sum(PSCELL_UE_0_ACT_SCELL_UL+ PSCELL_UE_1_ACT_SCELL_UL+ PSCELL_UE_2_ACT_SCELL_UL+ PSCELL_UE_3_ACT_SCELL_UL+ PSCELL_UE_4_ACT_SCELL_UL+ PSCELL_UE_5_ACT_SCELL_UL+ PSCELL_UE_6_ACT_SCELL_UL+ PSCELL_UE_7_ACT_SCELL_UL))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average number of activated SCells in uplink.

KPI.name: 5G SA Admission control rejection ratio due to lack of PUCCH resources
KPI.lformula: 100 * SA admission rejections due to lack of PUCCH resources / SA admission requestes
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the rejection ratio in admission control due to lack of PUCCH resources, in SA mode.

KPI.name: 5G NSA PDCP SDU data volume transmitted without repetitions in DL
KPI.lformula: NSA PDCP SDU dat vol tx w/o rep DL = DL PDCP SDU volume transmitted for NSA Bearer - QoS Group xx / 1000
KPI.nformula: (Sum(DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_01+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_02+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_03+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_04+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_05+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_06+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_07+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_08+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_09+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_10+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_11+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_12+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_13+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_14+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_15+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_16+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_17+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_18+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_19+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_20)/1000)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the PDCP SDU data volume transmitted in downlink direction towards the RLC layer in either gNB or eNB.

KPI.name: 5G NSA PDCP SDU data volume received without repetitions in UL
KPI.lformula: NSA PDCP SDU dat vol rx w/o rep UL = UL PDCP SDU volume transmitted for NSA Bearer - QoS Group xx / 1000
KPI.nformula: (Sum(UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_01+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_02+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_03+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_04+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_05+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_06+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_07+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_08+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_09+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_10+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_11+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_12+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_13+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_14+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_15+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_16+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_17+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_18+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_19+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_20)/1000)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the PDCP SDU data volume received via gNB or eNB in uplink direction.

KPI.name: 5G MAC SDU data volume transmitted in DL on DTCH
KPI.lformula: MAC SDU data vol trans DL DTCH = DL MAC SDU volume transmitted on DTCH]) / 1000000
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the MAC SDU data volume in MB of DTCH transmitted by first HARQ transmission for any Logical Channel ID for DRBs.

KPI.name: 5G MAC SDU data volume received in UL on DTCH
KPI.lformula: MAC SDU data vol rcvd UL DTCH = UL MAC SDU volume received on DTCH]) / 1000000
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the MAC SDU data volume in MB of DTCH received for any Logical Channel ID for DRBs. HARQ retransmissions are not counted.

KPI.name: 5G NSA PDCP SDU throughput (without repetitions) in DL
KPI.lformula: NSA PDCP SDU thp tx w/o rep DL = 8 * DL PDCP SDU volume transmitted for NSA Bearer - QoS Group xx / 1000 * Duration of the measurement period for the PDCP Report 1
KPI.nformula: (8*Sum(DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_01+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_02+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_03+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_04+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_05+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_06+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_07+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_08+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_09+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_10+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_11+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_12+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_13+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_14+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_15+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_16+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_17+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_18+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_19+ DL_VOL_TX_NSA_QOS_GRP_20)/(1000 * Sum(MEAS_PERIOD_NR_PDCP_REPORT1)))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows for NSA the PDCP SDU throughput in downlink direction. It is calculated from the DL PDCP SDU data volume sent via both eNB and gNB.

KPI.name: 5G NSA PDCP SDU throughput (without repetitions) in UL
KPI.lformula: NSA PDCP SDU thp tx w/o rep UL = 8 * UL PDCP SDU volume received for NSA Bearer - QoS Group xx / 1000 * Duration of the measurement period for the PDCP Report 1
KPI.nformula: (8*Sum(UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_01+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_02+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_03+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_04+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_05+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_06+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_07+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_08+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_09+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_10+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_11+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_12+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_13+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_14+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_15+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_16+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_17+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_18+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_19+ UL_VOL_RX_NSA_QOS_GRP_20)/(1000 * Sum(MEAS_PERIOD_NR_PDCP_REPORT1)))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows for NSA the PDCP SDU throughput in uplink direction. It is calculated from the UL PDCP SDU data volume received for NSA Bearer via gNB or eNB.

KPI.name: 5G BWP1 Configuration Success Ratio
KPI.lformula: BWP1 Conf SuccR= 100* (Number of RRC RECONFIGURATION COMPLETE messages received confirming configuration of first bandwidth part (BWP1))/ (number of RRC RECONFIGURATION messages sent for configuring first bandwidth part (BWP1))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows success ratio of RRC RECONFIGURATION procedure initiated configuring first bandwidth part (BWP1) on P(S)Cell.

KPI.name: 5G BWP2 Configuration Success Ratio
KPI.lformula: BWP2 Conf SuccR= 100* (Number of RRC RECONFIGURATION COMPLETE messages received confirming configuration of second bandwidth part (BWP2))/ (number of RRC RECONFIGURATION messages sent for configuring second bandwidth part (BWP2))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows success ratio of RRC RECONFIGURATION procedure initiated configuring second bandwidth part (BWP2) on P(S)Cell.

KPI.desc: This KPI shows the cell throughput on active PDSCH calculated at MAC layer by dividing the MAC PDU data volume transmitted on initial HARQ tx divided by the accumulated time of data slots used for PDSCH.This KPI gives info about RF capability as slots available for PDSCH (like UL Data slots or PRACH slots) are not considered in denominator. The SSB slots and TRS slots used for PDSCH are considered in denominator.

KPI.desc: This KPI shows the active cell throughput on PUSCH calculated at MAC layer by dividing the MAC PDU data volume transmitted on initial HARQ tx divided by the accumulated time of OFDM symbols used for PUSCH.

KPI.name: 5G SA PDCP SDU data volume transmitted in downlink
KPI.lformula: Sum of SA data volume sent in DL for QOS group 1 to 20/ 1000
KPI.nformula: Sum(DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_01+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_02+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_03+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_04+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_05+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_06+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_07+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_08+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_09+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_10+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_11+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_12+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_13+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_14+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_15+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_16+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_17+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_18+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_19+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_20)/ 1000
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the PDCP SDU data volume transmitted in downlink direction for SA Bearer via gNB. Discarded DL PDCP SDUs are not counted.

KPI.name: 5G SA PDCP SDU data volume received in uplink
KPI.lformula: Sum of SA data volume received in UL for QOS group 1 to 20/ 1000
KPI.nformula: Sum(UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_01+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_02+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_03+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_04+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_05+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_06+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_07+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_08+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_09+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_10+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_11+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_12+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_13+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_14+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_15+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_16+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_17+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_18+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_19+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_20)/ 1000
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the PDCP SDU data volume received in uplink direction for SA Bearer via gNB. Discarded UL PDCP SDUs are not counted.

KPI.name: 5G SA PDCP SDU throughput in downlink
KPI.lformula: 8* (Sum of SA data volume DL for QOS group 1 to 20 )/ ( 1000 * meas period in seconds)
KPI.nformula: 8 * Sum(DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_01+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_02+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_03+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_04+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_05+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_06+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_07+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_08+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_09+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_10+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_11+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_12+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_13+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_14+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_15+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_16+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_17+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_18+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_19+ DL_VOL_TX_SA_QOS_GRP_20)/ (1000 * Sum(MEAS_PERIOD_NR_PDCP_REPORT1))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the SA PDCP SDU throughput in downlink direction, calculated in the measurement period.

KPI.name: 5G SA PDCP SDU throughput in uplink
KPI.lformula: 8* (Sum of SA data volume UL for QOS group 1 to 20 )/ ( 1000 * meas period in seconds)
KPI.nformula: 8 * Sum(UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_01+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_02+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_03+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_04+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_05+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_06+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_07+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_08+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_09+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_10+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_11+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_12+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_13+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_14+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_15+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_16+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_17+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_18+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_19+ UL_VOL_RX_SA_QOS_GRP_20)/ (1000 * Sum(MEAS_PERIOD_NR_PDCP_REPORT1))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the SA PDCP SDU throughput in uplink direction, calculated in the measurement period.

KPI.name: 5G Scheduled user throughput in downlink
KPI.nformula: (8 * Sum(PDSCH_INI_VOL_64TBL_MCS00+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_64TBL_MCS01+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_64TBL_MCS02+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_64TBL_MCS03+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_64TBL_MCS04+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_64TBL_MCS05+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_256TBL_MCS05+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_256TBL_MCS06+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_256TBL_MCS07+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_256TBL_MCS08+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_256TBL_MCS09+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_256TBL_MCS10+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_256TBL_MCS11+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_05+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_06+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_07+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_08+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_09+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_10+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_11+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_12+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_13+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_14+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_15+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_16+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_17+ PDSCH_INI_VOL_LOWSE_MCS_18+ Sum(ACC_SCHED_UE_PDSCH_NOPAGING)))
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the scheduled user throughput in downlink calculated as the cell throughput divided by the total accumulated UEs scheduling time in DL.

KPI.name: 5G Average UE related SINR for PUSCH
KPI.lformula: Avg UE rel SINR PUSCH = Sum( (SINR of bin N) * ue_sinr_pusch_r1_level_bin N + ue_sinr_pusch_r2_level_bin N)) / Sum(ue_sinr_pusch_r1_level_bin N + ue_sinr_pusch_r2_level_bin N),N=[01, 02, ... ,25]
KPI.nformula: Sum(-8 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_01 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_01)-7 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_02 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_02)-5 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_03 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_03)-3 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_04 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_04)-1 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_05 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_05)+1 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_06 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_06)+3 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_07 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_07)+5 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_08 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_08)+7 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_09 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_09)+9 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_10 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_10)+11 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_11 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_11)+13 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_12 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_12)+15 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_13 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_13)+17 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_14 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_14)+19 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_15 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_15)+21 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_16 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_16)+23 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_17 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_17)+25 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_18 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_18)+27 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_19 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_19)+29 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_20 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_20)+31 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_21 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_21)+33 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_22 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_22)+35 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_23 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_23)+37 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_24 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_24)+38 * (UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_25 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_25))/Sum(UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_01 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_01+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_02 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_02+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_03 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_03+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_04 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_04+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_05 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_05+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_06 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_06+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_07 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_07+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_08 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_08+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_09 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_09+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_10 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_10+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_11 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_11+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_12 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_12+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_13 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_13+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_14 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_14+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_15 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_15+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_16 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_16+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_17 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_17+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_18 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_18+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_19 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_19+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_20 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_20+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_21 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_21+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_22 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_22+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_23 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_23+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_24 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_24+ UE_SINR_PUSCH_R1_LEVEL_25 + UE_SINR_PUSCH_R2_LEVEL_25)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average UE related Signal to Interference and Noise Ratio (SINR) for PUSCH (using Rank 1 or Rank2) measured in the gNB, calculated from the bin counters.

KPI.name: 5G Average MAC layer user throughput in downlink
KPI.lformula: Avg MAC user thp DL exc DRX sleep = Volume in Mbits / (Accumulated number of users with buffered data (sampled every 1 slots) * 1 slot * Slot duration)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average MAC layer user throughput in downlink. Calculated as MAC SDU volume with the accumulated UEs time with data in the buffer for DRBs excluding DRX sleep state.

KPI.name: 5G Average MAC layer user throughput in uplink
KPI.lformula: Cell user throughput = Volume in Mbits / (Accumulated number of users with buffered data (sampled every 1 slots) * 1 slots * Slot duration)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average MAC layer user throughput in uplink. It is calculated as Cell throughput divided by number of users.NR_5101b is only valid if ACC_UE_UL_DRB_DATA sample period is 1 slot.Cell user throughput = Volume in Mbits / (Accumulated number of users with buffered data (sampled every 1 slots) * 1 slots * Slot duration)

KPI.name: 5G SA SCell Change Success Ratio in PCell
KPI.lformula: 100 * SCell Change success / SCell Change attempt
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the SA SCell Change Success Ratio, counted in the PCell.

KPI.name: 5G SA SCell Change Attempts in PCell
KPI.lformula: SCell Change attempt
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the SA SCell Change Attempts, counted in the PCell.

KPI.name: 5G NSA Ratio of duplicated PDCP data PDUs sent over X2-U in downlink
KPI.lformula: 5G NSA Dupl to all DL PDCP data PDUs X2-U ratio = 100*(Num of dupl DL PDCP data PDUs via X2-U) / (Total num of DL PDCP data PDUs via X2-U)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the percentage of DL PDCP data PDUs sent over X2-U due to duplication, compared to total number of DL PDCP data PDUs sent over X2-U.PDU duplication is a mechanism for resetting inactivity timer for MeNB leg for NSA 3X mode to keep it alive even when LTE leg is out of data.

KPI.name: 5G Average MCS used in uplink for PUSCH with 256QAM table
KPI.lformula: Sum for all MCS(MCSx number * Init packets sent in MCSx with 256QAM table)/ All init packets sent in UL with 256QAM table
KPI.nformula: (0 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_00)+ 1 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_01)+ 2 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_02)+ 3 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_03)+ 4 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_04)+ 5 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_05)+ 6 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_06)+ 7 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_07)+ 8 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_08)+ 9 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_09)+ 10 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_10)+ 11 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_11)+ 12 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_12)+ 13 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_13)+ 14 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_14)+ 15 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_15)+ 16 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_16)+ 17 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_17)+ 18 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_18)+ 19 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_19)+ 20 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_20)+ 21 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_21)+ 22 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_22)+ 23 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_23)+ 24 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_24)+ 25 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_25)+ 26 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_26)+ 27 * Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_27))/Sum(PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_00+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_01+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_02+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_03+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_04+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_05+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_06+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_07+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_08+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_09+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_10+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_11+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_12+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_13+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_14+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_15+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_16+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_17+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_18+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_19+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_20+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_21+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_22+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_23+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_24+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_25+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_26+ PUSCH_CP_INI_256TBL_MCS_27)
KPI.desc: This KPI shows the average MCS used for uplink data transmissions over PUSCH with 256QAM MCS table.

KPI.name: 5G Spectral efficiency in downlink
KPI.lformula: Spectr effic DL= Data volume / (Time * bandwidth)Data volume[bits] = 8 * (PDSCH_INIRE_VOL_64TBL_MCx + PDSCH_INIRE_VOL_256TBL_MCx + PDSCH_INIRE_VOL_LOWSE_MCSx)Time[seconds] = PDSCH_OFDM_SYMBOLS_TIME / 1000000Badwith [Hz] = Number of PRBs * Bandwidth per PRB = (PRB_USED_PDSCH / (DATA_SLOT_PDSCH + SSB_SLOT_PDSCH + TRS_SLOT_PDSCH) * (180000 / ((DATA_SLOT_PDSCH_TIME+SSB_SLOT_PDSCH_TIME+TRS_SLOT_PDSCH_TIME) / 1000) / (DATA_SLOT_PDSCH + SSB_SLOT_PDSCH + TRS_SLOT_PDSCH)) =180 000 000 * (PRB_USED_PDSCH / (DATA_SLOT_PDSCH_TIME+SSB_SLOT_PDSCH_TIME+TRS_SLOT_PDSCH_TIME)

KPI.desc: This KPI shows the Spectral efficiency calculated from the initial transmission or retransmission on PDSCH and the PDSCH OFDM symbols time.

KPI.name: 5G Spectral efficiency in uplink
KPI.lformula: Spectr effic UL = Data volume / (Time * bandwidth) Data volume[bits] = 8 * (PUSCH_INIRE_VOL_64TBL_MCx +PUSCH_DFT_INRE_VOL_64TBL_MCSx + PUSCH_CP_INRE_VOL_256TBL_MCSx + PUSCH_CP_INRE_VOL_LOWSE_MCSx) Time[seconds] = PUSCH_OFDM_SYMBOLS_TIME / 1000000 Badwith [Hz] = Number of PRBs * Bandwidth per PRB = (PRB_USED_PUSCH / (DATA_SLOT_PUSCH+ PRACH_SLOTS_PUSCH)) * (180000 / ((DATA_SLOT_PUSCH_TIME + PRACH_SLOTS_PUSCH_TIME) / 1000) / (DATA_SLOT_PUSCH+ PRACH_SLOTS_PUSCH)) = 180 000 000 * (PRB_USED_PUSCH / (DATA_SLOT_PUSCH_TIME + PRACH_SLOTS_PUSCH_TIME))
